name: King and Dungeness Crab Meat
recipeIngredient: Blue crab meat (frozen for best quality)
recipeInstructions:
1. Keep live crabs on ice until ready to process.
2. Wash crabs thoroughly using several changes of cold water.
3. Simmer crabs 20 minutes in water containing 1/4 cup lemon juice and 2 tablespoons salt per gallon (up to 1 cup salt if desired).
4. Cool crabs in cold water, drain, remove the back shell, and then remove meat from body and claws.
5. Soak meat for 2 minutes in cold water containing 2 cups lemon juice or 4 cups white vinegar and 2 tablespoons salt per gallon (up to 1 cup salt if desired).
6. Drain and squeeze out excess moisture.
7. Fill half-pint jars with 6 ounces of meat and pint jars with 12 ounces, leaving 1-inch headspace.
8. Add 1/2 teaspoon citric acid or 2 tablespoons lemon juice per half-pint jar, or 1 teaspoon citric acid or 4 tablespoons lemon juice per pint jar.
9. Add hot water, leaving 1-inch headspace.
10. Adjust lids and process according to the recommendations in Table 1 or Table 2 based on the canning method used.
Table 1. Recommended processing time for King and Dungeness Crab Meat in a dial-gauge pressure canner.
- Jar Size: Half-pints, Pints
- Processing Time: 70 minutes (half-pints), 80 minutes (pints)
- Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 2,000 ft: 11 lb
Table 2. Recommended processing time for King and Dungeness Crab Meat in a weighted-gauge pressure canner.
- Jar Size: Half-pints, Pints
- Processing Time: 70 minutes (half-pints), 80 minutes (pints)
- Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 10 lb
- Above 1,000 ft: 15 lb
